contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC, NERC, Northern Eurasia Finals (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Official NERC tutorial; used to confirm the one-opening reduction, extreme left/right door positions, and multiset sweep.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Given movable door segments on several lanes, maximize the size of a single opening after shifting doors within each lane.
annotation.constraints: The total number of doors is large enough that candidate openings must be scanned after sorting rather than checking all pairs.
annotation.core_idea: It is enough to consider one opening: multiple openings can be merged by moving intervening doors to one side. Move all doors as far left as possible and record each door's rightmost point R_{i,j}; move all doors as far right as possible and record each door's leftmost point L_{i,j}. Any optimal opening has left border at some R and right border at some L. Sweep increasing R: doors with R <= current left border are moved left, all others stay right. Maintain the minimum L among doors still on the right in an ordered multiset; current opening length is minRightL - maxLeftR. Update the answer during the sweep.
annotation.complexity: O(K log K), where K is the total number of doors plus lane boundaries.
tricks: sweep candidate left borders with right-side minimum

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Precompute R and L extreme coordinates, sort by R, and sweep while an ordered multiset stores the smallest remaining L.

tags.evidence: The solution scans candidate left borders in sorted order while maintaining the best compatible right border.
During the sweep it must delete door candidates and query the current minimum L among doors still on the right.
tags.description: Balanced ordered multiset supporting duplicate keys, insertion/deletion, and min/max or predecessor/successor queries.
tags.created_reason: Several sweep-line problems need duplicate-aware ordered sets rather than a heap or hash map.

tags.evidence: All candidate border events are processed in increasing R order.
The key reduction is that an optimal border is realized by moving doors fully left or fully right.
tags.description: Show an optimum can be achieved by pushing objects to an extreme feasible position.
tags.created_reason: This exchange/reduction pattern is useful in interval packing and geometry-like optimization problems.
